courses.title: MCP: Build Rich-Context AI Apps with Anthropic

content_chunks.course_title: MCP: Build Rich-Context AI Apps with Anthropic
content_chunks.content: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a revolutionary approach to building AI applications. It allows for rich context sharing between different AI models and systems, enabling more sophisticated and contextually aware applications.

content_chunks.course_title: MCP: Build Rich-Context AI Apps with Anthropic
content_chunks.content: The key benefits of MCP include improved context retention, better model coordination, enhanced application performance, and reduced hallucination in AI responses.

content_chunks.course_title: MCP: Build Rich-Context AI Apps with Anthropic
content_chunks.content: To build your first MCP server, you need to understand the protocol specifications and implement the required interfaces. This includes setting up connection handlers, message routing, and context management.

content_chunks.course_title: MCP: Build Rich-Context AI Apps with Anthropic
content_chunks.content: MCP servers can be implemented in various programming languages. Python is a popular choice due to its extensive ecosystem and ease of development.

RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) combines information retrieval with text generation to create more accurate and contextual AI responses. It addresses the limitation of large language models in accessing up-to-date information.
The RAG process involves three main steps: retrieval of relevant documents, augmentation of the prompt with retrieved context, and generation of the final response using the augmented prompt.

Vector databases are essential for RAG systems. They store document embeddings and enable semantic search, allowing the system to find relevant information based on meaning rather than just keyword matching.
Modern chatbot architecture typically consists of multiple components: natural language understanding (NLU), dialogue management, natural language generation (NLG), and integration layers.
Context management in chatbots is crucial for maintaining conversation flow. This includes tracking conversation history, user preferences, and session state across multiple interactions.
